title: Byrsonima volubilis
taxonomy: Byrsonima volubilis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and within the plant kingdom, it belongs to the division Magnoliophyta. It is further classified into the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with two-celled anthers. The order Malpighiales is also a part of its classification, a group of plants with simple leaves and small, four- or five-merous flowers. Within the family Malpighiaceae, Byrsonima volubilis is a member of this family, which is characterized by its numerous stamens and four- or five-merous flowers. Byrsonima volubilis is commonly known as the "creeping byrsonima" and is native to tropical regions.

Its leaves and other parts are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial for treating certain health conditions. intro: Byrsonima volubilis, commonly known as the "Uvaia" or "Occhro", is a climbing shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Byrsonima volubilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Byrsonima volubilis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or as a tincture, to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
